---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **forecasting & evaluation** course, taught in the second half of the SISMID week (**Wednesday midday to Friday**).
It is paired with the companion [nowcasting course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-nowcasting/).

The schedule follows the standard SISMID day: modules run 09.00–10.30, 11.00–12.30, 13.30–15.00 and 15.30–17.00, with coffee breaks at 10.30 and 15.00 and lunch 12.30–13.30.
Session lengths follow the times these sessions took last year; the remaining slots (two on Friday) are held open for new or expanded sessions (see the open issues).

# Day 1 — Wednesday 15 July (afternoon): Forecasting concepts and models

The forecasting course begins after the Wednesday-midday handover from the nowcasting course.

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

Wednesday: 13.30–14.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors
-   [Getting started](../getting-started): getting set up, and a recap of the nowcasting course

## Session 1: Forecasting concepts

Wednesday: 14.00–14.45

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-forecasting): forecasting as an epidemiological problem
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-concepts): extending a model into the future and visualising a forecast
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 15.00–15.30*

## Session 2: Improving forecasting models

Wednesday: 15.30–17.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/improving-forecasting-models): ARIMA concepts and transformations
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-models): ACF plots, transformations, and seasonality
-   Wrap up

# Day 2 — Thursday 16 July: Evaluation, ensembles and hubs

Thursday: 09.00–09.15 — Day 1 review

## Session 3: Forecast evaluation

Thursday: 09.15–10.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecast-evaluation): scoring rules and metrics
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-evaluation): evaluating forecasts with a range of metrics
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 10.30–11.00*

## Session 4: Forecasting with ensembles

Thursday: 11.00–12.30

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-ensembles): strategies for collating and combining models
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-ensembles): evaluating methods for ensemble forecasts
-   Wrap up

*Lunch 12.30–13.30*

## Session 5: Evaluating real-world outbreak forecasts

Thursday: 13.30–15.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-real-world-forecasts): hub concepts and data structures
-   [Practice](../sessions/real-world-forecasts): evaluation and ensembles with an existing hub
-   Wrap up

*Coffee break 15.00–15.30*

## Session 6: Local hub playground

Thursday: 15.30–17.00

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/hub-playground): why a local hub?
-   [Practice](../sessions/hub-playground): getting stuck in with a local hub
-   Wrap up

# Day 3 — Friday 17 July: New material, research talks and wrap-up

Friday: 09.00–09.15 — Day 2 review

## Session 7: New session (to be written)

Friday: 09.15–10.30

-   Held open for a new or expanded forecasting session — see the open issues for candidates.

*Coffee break 10.30–11.00*

## Session 8: New session (to be written)

Friday: 11.00–12.30

-   A second new or expanded session slot — see the open issues for candidates.

*Lunch 12.30–13.30*

## Session 9: Research talks

Friday: 13.30–15.00

-   Research talk: [flusion](../sessions/slides/flusion.pdf)
-   Research talk: [allocation score](../sessions/slides/allocation-score.pdf)

*Coffee break 15.00–15.30*

## Session 10: Summary and wrap-up

Friday: 15.30–17.00

-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the course
-   Final discussion and [further reading](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion)
